message:
fix: handle Python triple-quoted strings in comment stripping

The stripHashCommentsPython function was processing files line-by-line
without tracking multi-line string state. This caused `#` characters
inside triple-quoted docstrings to be incorrectly treated as comments
and stripped from the output.

Example of the bug:
```python
def foo():
    """
    This docstring has a # character  <- was incorrectly stripped!
    """
    pass
```

The fix rewrites the function to process the entire file character-by-
character, properly tracking state for:
- Triple-double quotes (""")
- Triple-single quotes (''')
- Regular single and double quotes
- Escape sequences within strings

The function now correctly preserves `#` characters inside any string
type while still stripping actual Python comments.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
